title: Fractions of a Whole
essential_question: How can a fraction name part of a whole?
strategies: count total equal parts for denominator

vertical_alignment.prerequisite: G3 U8_understand_fractions/L3_unit_fractions_of_a_whole — Unit Fractions of a Whole (3.NF.A.1)
vertical_alignment.successor: G3 U8_understand_fractions/L5_fractions_on_a_number_line — Fractions on a Number Line (3.NF.A.2)

pretest.question: A circle is divided into 4 equal parts. 3 parts are shaded. What fraction is shaded?
pretest.explanation: 3 shaded parts out of 4 equal parts = 3/4.

feedback.incorrect: 3 shaded parts out of 4 equal parts = 3/4.

hints: Division splits a total into equal groups. Use the inverse multiplication fact.

A.note: 3 shaded parts out of 4 equal parts = 3/4.

B.note: 3 shaded parts out of 4 equal parts = 3/4.

D.note: 3 shaded parts out of 4 equal parts = 3/4.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: A rectangle is divided into 6 equal parts. 2 parts are shaded. What fraction is shaded?

feedback.incorrect: 2 out of 6 equal parts = 2/6.

hints: Division splits a total into equal groups. Use the inverse multiplication fact.

D.note: 2 out of 6 equal parts = 2/6.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Which fraction has 5 as the numerator?

feedback.incorrect: In 5/8, the numerator (top number) is 5.

hints: Numerator (top) = parts taken. Denominator (bottom) = total equal parts.

D.note: In 5/8, the numerator (top number) is 5.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: A rectangle is divided into 8 equal parts. 5 parts are shaded. What fraction is NOT shaded?

feedback.incorrect: 8 − 5 = 3 parts not shaded → 3/8.

hints: Division splits a total into equal groups. Use the inverse multiplication fact.

D.note: 8 − 5 = 3 parts not shaded → 3/8.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Which fraction is equal to 1 whole?
pretest.explanation: When the numerator equals the denominator, the fraction equals 1 whole. 6/6 = 1.

feedback.incorrect: When the numerator equals the denominator, the fraction equals 1 whole. 6/6 = 1.

hints: Numerator (top) = parts taken. Denominator (bottom) = total equal parts.

A.note: When the numerator equals the denominator, the fraction equals 1 whole. 6/6 = 1.

C.note: When the numerator equals the denominator, the fraction equals 1 whole. 6/6 = 1.

D.note: When the numerator equals the denominator, the fraction equals 1 whole. 6/6 = 1.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Naming Fractions
learn.content: To name a fraction: count the shaded (or chosen) parts — that is the numerator (top). Count the total equal parts — that is the denominator (bottom). Example: 3 out of 4 parts shaded = 3/4.

learn.title: More Than a Unit Fraction
learn.content: A unit fraction has 1 as the numerator (like 1/4). When more than 1 part is shaded, the numerator is greater than 1 (like 2/4 or 3/4).

learn.title: The Whole as a Fraction
learn.content: If ALL parts are shaded, the fraction equals the whole. 4/4 = 1 whole, 6/6 = 1 whole, 8/8 = 1 whole.

try.question: A rectangle is divided into 3 equal parts. 2 parts are shaded. What fraction is shaded?
hints: Think about what fractions of a whole means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

practice_r2.question: Which fraction is greater than 1/2?
practice_r2.explanation: 3/4 means 3 out of 4 parts, which is more than half (2 out of 4). The others are less than or equal to 1/2.

practice_r3.question: A rectangle is divided into 8 equal parts. Lily shades more than 4/8 but fewer than 7/8. Which could be the fraction she shaded?
practice_r3.explanation: More than 4/8 and fewer than 7/8 → 5/8 or 6/8. 5/8 is the matching choice.
hints: Think about what fractions of a whole means.

practice_r3.question: Two shapes are each divided into equal parts. Shape A has 3/4 shaded. Shape B has 3/6 shaded. Which statement is true?
choices: A) Shape A and Shape B have the same fraction shaded
B) Shape A has a greater fraction shaded than Shape B
C) Shape B has a greater fraction shaded than Shape A
practice_r3.explanation: 3/4 = 0.75 and 3/6 = 0.5, so Shape A has a greater fraction shaded.
hints: Think about what fractions of a whole means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

A.note: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

C.note: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

D.note: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Jake says 2/4 and 4/8 are different amounts. Is he correct?
choices: A) Yes, because the numbers are different
B) Yes, because 4/8 has more parts
C) No, both fractions name the same amount
practice_r3.explanation: 2/4 = 1/2 and 4/8 = 1/2. They name the same amount even though the numbers look different.
hints: Think about what fractions of a whole means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how fractions of a whole works.
